articles.headline: Boise State, Colorado State, Utah State sue Mountain West
articles.description: Boise State, Colorado State and Utah State filed an updated lawsuit arguing the Mountain West and commissioner Gloria Nevarez willfully disregarded the league's bylaws by "intentionally and fraudulently" depriving the schools of their membership rights.

articles.headline: 'Healthy, vibrant' Sanders expects Colorado to 'win differently'
articles.description: After a recent cancer scare, Colorado coach Deion Sanders says he's feeling "healthy and vibrant" ahead of his team's season opener against Georgia Tech on Aug. 29 in Boulder.

images.name: Deion Sanders announces bladder cancer battle
images.caption: Colorado coach Deion Sanders and his medical crew reveal that he battled and beat bladder cancer.

articles.headline: Where is 'College GameDay'? 2025 schedule, locations, recaps
articles.description: It's time for a new season of "College GameDay" on ESPN! Check out the event hub page for weekly site details, recaps and other information.

articles.headline: LSU QB Garrett Nussmeier dealing with patellar tendinitis
articles.description: LSU quarterback Garrett Nussmeier is dealing with patellar tendinitis in his knee after aggravating the issue in practice this week, according to coach Brian Kelly.

article.headline: Barkley helps No. 10 Penn State beat Indiana 45-31
article.description: Saquon Barkley ran for two go-ahead touchdowns in the fourth quarter, helping No. 10 Penn State beat Indiana 45-31 on Saturday.
article.linkText: Barkley helps No. 10 Penn State beat Indiana 45-31

related.headline: Big-play offense keeps Penn State in mix for New Year's Six
related.title: Big-play offense keeps Penn State in mix for New Year's Six
related.linkText: Big-play offense keeps Penn State in mix for New Year's Six

images.name: Penn State at Indiana (McSorley passing) [576x324]
images.caption: Trace McSorley overcame two interceptions to help rally Penn State down the stretch.

categories.description: Penn State Nittany Lions @ Indiana Hoosiers

event.description: Penn State Nittany Lions @ Indiana Hoosiers

keywords: Penn State Nittany Lions @ Indiana Hoosiers
article.story:
BLOOMINGTON, Ind. -- Saquon Barkley ran for two go-ahead touchdowns in the fourth quarter, helping No. 10 Penn State beat Indiana 45-31 on Saturday.

Barkley was held to 20 yards on 20 carries though the first three quarters. But the Big Ten's top rusher stepped up in the final period, helping the Nittany Lions (8-2, 6-1, No. 12 AP) keep pace with No. 3 Michigan in the East Division with their sixth consecutive victory.

First, Barkley found a hole for a 4-yard TD run that made it 28-24. After Indiana regained the lead on Richard Lagow's 40-yard touchdown pass to Nick Westbrook, Barkley ran over a defender for a 2-yard score with 3:58 left.

"What you can't do when you're struggling in the run game is abort it, especially with a young offensive line," Penn State coach James Franklin said. "It was a gutsy, gutsy win on the road."

Penn State sealed the victory with a 9-yard fumble return for a TD with 29 seconds left.

Barkley finished with 60 yards on 33 carries. Trace McSorley was 16 of 30 for 332 yards with two TD passes and two interceptions.

Indiana (5-5, 3-4) had won two in a row. Lagow was 23 of 40 for 292 yards and two TDs, and Devine Redding ran 23 times for 108 yards and two scores.

"As hard as we played, as much as we played, we've got to play better," Indiana coach Kevin Wilson said. "And I've got to coach better down the stretch."

THE TAKEAWAY

Penn State: The Nittany Lions maintained their long-shot division hopes, but still need help. They dropped their conference opener to the unbeaten Wolverines.

Indiana: The Hoosiers blew a golden opportunity for a signature win. It would have been Indiana's second Top 25 victory this season and secured bowl eligibility with two games to play. Next up is Michigan in Ann Arbor before finishing with rival Purdue in the regular-season finale.

KEY NUMBERS

Indiana lost five fumbles, four in the first half. Penn State made it 14-7 after recovering a muffed punt at Indiana's 6-yard line. Penn State committed two turnovers and the Hoosiers scored their second touchdown with one minute left in the first half after picking off McSorley.

INJURY REPORT

Penn State's already depleted offensive line took another hit when left tackle Paris Palmer left in the first quarter with an undisclosed injury. Cornerback Jordan Smith was sent off the field by the refs after the first play of the fourth quarter to enter the concussion protocol.

POLL IMPLICATIONS

Penn State: The Nittany Lions could become the sixth Big Ten team to crack the top 10 this season.

Indiana: The Hoosiers haven't been ranked in the Top 25 since 1994 -- and this loss won't help them end the longest current drought for a power-five school.

UP NEXT

Penn State: Things should get easier next weekend when the Nittany Lions visit Rutgers, the last Big Ten team winless in conference play.

Indiana: After playing four ranked teams in their last seven games, the Hoosiers face an even more daunting task next weekend. Michigan has won the last 20 in the series.

videos.headline: McSorley bowls over Indiana defender for TD
videos.description: Penn State QB Trace McSorley doesn't let an Indiana defender get in his way as McSorley rushes for a 10-yard touchdown.

videos.headline: Penn State punter saves play after fumbling snap
videos.description: Freshman punter Blake Gillikin fumbles the snap but makes up for it as he maintains composure picking up the ball, managing to get off a kick.

videos.headline: Penn State forces Indiana fumble
videos.description: Indiana's Mitchell Paige catches the screen pass and then fumbles, Penn State recovers

videos.headline: Indiana's Redding finds the end zone for 12-yard TD

videos.headline: Flea flicker puts Penn State deep in Indiana territory
videos.description: Penn State's trick play puts the Nittany Lions on the Indiana 3-yard line in the fourth quarter.

videos.headline: Barkley puts Penn State in front
videos.description: Saquon Barkley scores in the fourth quarter giving Penn State the lead over Indiana.

videos.headline: Indiana quickly takes back the lead with 40-yard TD
videos.description: Nick Westbrook hauls in a 40-yard pass from Richard Lagow to take back the lead from Penn State in the fourth quarter.

videos.headline: Barkley's 2nd TD gives Penn State lead again
videos.description: Saquon Barkley bull rushes his way into the end zone to give Penn State the lead once again over Indiana.
